new = yes | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | relatives = [[Tyla Hepi]] (son) }} }} '''Brad Hepi''' (born 11 February 1968) is a New Zealand [[rugby league]] player who played professionally in both England and Australia. ==Early years== Hepi attended [[Kelston Boys' High School]] and captained their first XV [[rugby union]] team. ==Playing career== Hepi played in the [[1986 Pacific Cup|1986]] and [[1988 Pacific Cup]]s for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]].{{cite book |last =John Coffey, Bernie Wood |title = 100 years: Māori rugby league, 1908-2008 |publisher =Huia Publishers |year = 2008 |url = https://books.google.com/books?id=nklWo8vw-iIC |isbn =978-1-86969-331-2}} In 1989 he moved to England, joining [[Carlisle RLFC]].''Oldham v Carlisle Official Matchday Programme'' Sunday, 8 March 1992. p.15 He then had a very successful spell with [[Workington Town]] which saw the club promoted from the third division to the first in two seasons, winning the Divisional Premiership against London Broncos along the way. Town initially struggled in the top flight and found themselves at the bottom of the league at Christmas 1994. But an early-January 36-10 win over Halifax, with Hepi man of the match, was the turning point. From there, they rose from 16th to 9th – a position which saw them qualify for the first Super League season in 1996. At the end of the 1994-95 campaign, Hepi moved to Australia, signing with the [[Illawarra Steelers]] for their [[1996 Illawarra Steelers season|1996 season]]. He returned to Derwent Park in 1997, but couldn't prevent Town's relegation to the professional game's bottom tier. Nevertheless, for what he did in his first spell at the club, Hepi is fondly remembered in Workington. In 1998 he played for [[Hull FC]] in [[Super League III]]. Hepi then had spells with the [[Castleford Tigers]] (1999 and 2001), [[Featherstone Rovers]] (2000), [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] (2000) and [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] (2001) before retiring.[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Brad-Hepi/summary.html Brad Hepi] ''rugbyleagueproject.org'' Although retired a few years Hepi has made a few cameo appearances for the [[Gloucestershire All Golds]] in the 2013 season.[http://www.rlcm.com.au/latest-news/20421-rugby-league Brad Hepi coaching Gloucestershire All Golds] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150415041329/http://www.rlcm.com.au/latest-news/20421-rugby-league# |date=15 April 2015 }} ''rlcm.com.au'', 30 November 2012 {{s-start}} {{S-sports}} {{Succession box | before = | after = [[Steve McCormack]]
2014 | title = Coach
{{leagueicon|gloucestershire|size=18}}
[[Gloucestershire All Golds]] | years = 2013-2014 }} {{S-end}} ==Later years== Hepi was appointed coach of the [[Gloucestershire All Golds|Gloucestershire All-Golds]] for the 2013 season. He coached the side in their first season of the Kingstone Press Championship 1 league where they reached bottom place. In the 2014 kingstonpress championship one season he saw the side into the first seven-game before stepping down from the position, stating he had taken the side as far as he could. His son, [[Tyla Hepi]], has played in the [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition for the [[Point Chevalier Pirates]].[http://www.theaucklander.co.nz/news/into-his-league/1065859/ Into his league] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120125014121/http://www.theaucklander.co.nz/news/into-his-league/1065859/ |date=25 January 2012 }} ''theaucklander.co.nz'', 11 August 2011 He also played under his father at the [[Gloucestershire All Golds|Gloucestershire All-Golds]] whilst on loan from Hull Kingston Rovers. ==References== {{Reflist|2}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hepi, Brad}} [[Category:1968 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Carlisle RLFC players]] [[Category:Castleford Tigers players]] [[Category:Dewsbury Rams players]] [[Category:Doncaster R.L.F.C. players]] [[Category:Featherstone Rovers players]] [[Category:Glenora Bears players]] [[Category:Gloucestershire All Golds coaches]] [[Category:Gloucestershire All Golds players]] [[Category:Hull F.C. players]] [[Category:Illawarra Steelers players]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in England]] [[Category:New Zealand expatriate rugby league players in Australia]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league players]] [[Category:New Zealand Māori rugby league team players]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league coaches]] [[Category:New Zealand rugby league players]] [[Category:People educated at Kelston Boys' High School]] [[Category:Place of birth missing (living people)]] [[Category:Rugby league hookers]] [[Category:Salford RLFC players]] [[Category:Sheffield Eagles players]] [[Category:Workington Town players]] [[Category:Rugby articles needing expert attention]]
